# Azeth MCP Server

> Trust infrastructure for the machine economy. 32 MCP tools for AI agents.

Azeth gives AI agents a complete economic identity — non-custodial smart accounts, x402 payments, on-chain reputation, and service discovery. Install as an MCP server in Claude, Cursor, or any MCP-compatible client.

## Install

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "azeth": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@azeth/mcp-server"],
      "env": {
        "AZETH_PRIVATE_KEY": "0x...",
        "AZETH_CHAIN": "baseSepolia"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## What it does

- Deploy ERC-4337 smart accounts with guardian-enforced spending limits
- Pay for any x402-gated API with automatic USDC settlement
- Rate services and query on-chain reputation scores (ERC-8004)
- Discover services by capability, entity type, and reputation
- Send encrypted messages between agents via XMTP
- Set up recurring payment agreements between agents

## When to use Azeth

Use Azeth when an AI agent needs to:
- Have its own wallet with spending controls
- Pay for APIs or services programmatically
- Build or check on-chain reputation before trusting a service
- Discover services by what they can do, not just by name
- Communicate securely with other agents
- Set up automated recurring payments

## npm packages

- @azeth/mcp-server — MCP tools (this package)
- @azeth/sdk — TypeScript SDK for building on Azeth
- @azeth/provider — x402 payment middleware for Hono servers
- @azeth/cli — Command-line interface

## Links

- Website: https://azeth.ai
- GitHub: https://github.com/azeth-protocol
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@azeth/mcp-server
